rows.item: 学習意欲・授業参加度

summary:
授業実施方法：オンライン同期型
EPIC is structured to satisfy the needs of upper-intermediate students. EPIC is a package of six language-focused courses (four skills-based courses and two applied communication courses) taken over one semester to ensure a high degree of intensity in English study. Each of these courses is further subdivided into project-based modules. EPIC covers language knowledge, all four language skills, and a variety of applied activities and projects. Each of the three EPIC instructors are responsible for four modules during the semester and students receive instruction in the full range of English competencies. Extensive Reading (ER) and self-access learning are built into the EPIC Program to encourage greater learner autonomy in language study. Three native-speaker instructors closely coordinate curriculum, course material, and assessment.
aims: The EPIC course aims to equip students with the necessary language skills, cultural understanding, and autonomy to excel in both academic and practical English language contexts. The combination of structured courses, applied projects, and extensive reading ensures a holistic approach to language acquisition and proficiency development.
goals: Foster a habit of extensive reading to improve language comprehension and expand vocabulary.
Develop critical thinking, problem-solving, and collaborative skills through hands-on projects.
Improve proficiency in all four language skills – listening, speaking, reading, and writing.

EPIC Course and Diploma Exams

prereq: Students are selected for EPIC based on first year English results or English ability. A speaking and writing assessment will be done in the first two classes of the EPIC Programme to determine eligibility to take this special course. Students who are interested in EPIC should consult with the General Education administrative staff or Prof. Sean Burgoine prior to enrollment. There is a special pre-registration procedure for enrollment in EPIC.
prep: Students should keep a folder of work proving they have done all the preparation for all project work (spoken activities and all written assignments). Your folder should show that you do self-motivated study in addition to the tasks set by the EPIC teacher. Students will be expected to do a significant amount of work outside of class hours to successfully complete this special intensive EPIC Programme.
teachers: ◎ ショーン バーゴイン
